message:
Add RCH tool adapter implementation

Implements binary detection and worker availability checking for
Remote Compilation Helper (rch). Follows DCG adapter pattern with:
- Version checking and compatibility validation (min v0.1.0)
- Worker status monitoring with 30s TTL caching
- Auto-selection of healthiest worker by load
- Comprehensive test suite

Closes: bd-1ghcp

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1g5t8","title":"Task: Extend balanced strategy with assignment tracking","description":"## Overview
Balanced strategy already exists. This task extends it to use **real assignment tracking data** (from `bd-92e1`) and improves tie‑breakers without changing the public strategy name.

## Why
Current balanced behavior assumes assignment counts are populated. Without tracking, all counts are zero, so the strategy degenerates to simple capability scoring. We want true load‑aware balancing once tracking is available.

## Changes (extend, don’t replace)
- Wire assignment tracking into `assign.Agent.Assignments` (live counts per agent).
- Add optional tie‑breakers:
  1) fewer active assignments
  2) idle status
  3) least‑recent assignment timestamp
  4) best capability score
- Keep deterministic ordering for identical inputs.

## Inputs
- Assignment tracker state (`bd-92e1`).
- Agent health/availability (if available in current context).

## Outputs
- Deterministic assignment map (pane -> bead).
- Robot output includes per‑agent load snapshot and reasons (if present).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Balanced strategy still returns consistent assignments for the same inputs.
- Assignment counts reflect real state when tracking is enabled.
- Graceful fallback when tracking data is unavailable.
- Tests updated to cover load‑aware tie‑breakers.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:57:40.168441307Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:31:07.660098480Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:31:07.660054547Z","close_reason":"Implemented load-aware balanced strategy with tie-breakers: 1) fewer active assignments, 2) capability score, 3) least-recently assigned, 4) lower pane index for determinism. Added tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1g5t8","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:07:15.372750073Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g5t8","depends_on_id":"bd-k3zq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:04:58.914638245Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1g9wv","title":"Feature: Robot Status Enrichments","description":"## Overview

Enrich the existing `ntm --robot-status` output with critical operational data that
is currently missing but essential for automated swarm management.

## Background

The current --robot-status returns basic pane information (index, agent_type, is_active)
but lacks the data needed to:
- Detect rate-limited panes
- Identify hung processes
- Know which PID to kill for restart
- Track output freshness for stuck detection

## Proposed Schema Addition

Each pane object in the status output should include:

```json
{
  "index": 2,
  "agent_type": "claude",
  "pid": 12345,                    // Shell PID (already available)
  "child_pid": 12346,              // NEW: The actual agent process PID
  "last_output_ts": "2026-01-19T...", // NEW: Last output timestamp
  "rate_limit_detected": false,    // NEW: Pattern match on recent output
  "process_state": "running",      // NEW: running|sleeping|zombie|dead
  "memory_mb": 450,                // NEW: Process memory usage
  "output_lines_since_last": 42    // NEW: Activity indicator
}
```

## Justification for Each Field

1. **child_pid**: Required for kill -9 when soft restart fails. Currently operators
   must run `pgrep -P <shell_pid>` manually.

2. **last_output_ts**: Enables timeout-based stuck detection without external state.
   "No output for 5 minutes" = likely stuck.

3. **rate_limit_detected**: Pattern match for "You've hit your limit" or similar.
   This is the #1 failure mode in practice. Must be detected programmatically.

4. **process_state**: From /proc/<pid>/status. Zombie processes need different handling
   than running ones.

5. **memory_mb**: Detect runaway processes consuming excessive memory.

6. **output_lines_since_last**: Quick activity indicator without timestamp parsing.

## Implementation Notes

- child_pid: Use pgrep -P or parse /proc/<pid>/task
- last_output_ts: Track during capture-pane operations
- rate_limit_detected: Grep last N lines of pane output
- process_state: Read /proc/<pid>/status State field
- memory_mb: Read /proc/<pid>/status VmRSS field
- These should be gathered efficiently (single pass where possible)

## Performance Considerations

- Status is called frequently; keep it fast
- Pattern matching for rate limits should be bounded (last 50 lines)
- Process state reads are cheap (procfs)
- Consider caching with short TTL if needed

## Testing Requirements

- Unit tests for each field extraction
- E2E tests with actual stuck/rate-limited agents
- Performance benchmarks for large swarms (20+ panes)","notes":"Robot Status Enrichments feature is complete. All essential fields implemented: rate_limit_detected (bd-1mcvs), child_pid (bd-36dd7), last_output_ts (bd-3hxdl), process_state (bd-3ivl8), memory_mb (bd-lo0ko). The output_lines_since_last field (bd-33tuj) was deliberately skipped for performance reasons per existing comment in status_enrich.go.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:13:19.647496277Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T02:41:21.086289872Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T02:41:21.086191316Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1g9wv","depends_on_id":"bd-258a1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:13:19.725383416Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1gbt5","title":"Add ntm assign --reassign for moving bead between agents","description":"## Overview
Allow users to move an assignment from one agent to another without losing tracking state.

## Use Cases
1. Agent is stuck and user wants to give bead to a faster agent
2. User realizes a bead is better suited for a different agent type
3. Load balancing - redistribute work mid-session

## Usage
```bash
# Reassign bead to a different pane
ntm assign myproject --reassign bd-xyz --to-pane=4

# Reassign to specific agent type (picks idle agent of that type)
ntm assign myproject --reassign bd-xyz --to-type=codex

# Reassign with new prompt
ntm assign myproject --reassign bd-xyz --to-pane=4 --prompt="Continue from where the previous agent left off"
```

## Workflow
1. Validate bead is currently assigned
2. Get current assignment info
3. Update assignment status to "reassigned"
4. Create new assignment for target agent
5. Release file reservations from old agent (if any)
6. Create file reservations for new agent (if any)
7. Send prompt to new agent
8. Return reassignment result

## Output (Standard Envelope)
```json
{
  "command": "assign",
  "subcommand": "reassign",
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "reassigned": {
      "bead_id": "bd-xyz",
      "bead_title": "Implement feature X",
      "pane": 4,
      "agent_type": "codex",
      "agent_name": "BlueLake",
      "status": "assigned",
      "prompt_sent": true,
      "assigned_at": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
      "previous_pane": 2,
      "previous_agent": "GreenCastle",
      "previous_agent_type": "claude",
      "previous_status": "working",
      "file_reservations_transferred": true
    }
  },
  "warnings": [],
  "error": null
}
```

## Error Output
```json
{
  "command": "assign",
  "subcommand": "reassign",
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
  "success": false,
  "data": null,
  "warnings": [],
  "error": {
    "code": "TARGET_BUSY",
    "message": "pane 4 already has assignment bd-abc",
    "details": {
      "current_bead": "bd-abc",
      "current_status": "working"
    }
  }
}
```

## Error Codes
- NOT_ASSIGNED: Bead doesn't have an active assignment
- TARGET_BUSY: Target pane already has an assignment
- PANE_NOT_FOUND: Target pane doesn't exist
- NO_IDLE_AGENT: No idle agent of specified type

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reassign bead to specific pane
- [ ] Reassign by agent type (auto-select idle)
- [ ] Transfer file reservations via Agent Mail
- [ ] Update assignment store correctly
- [ ] Optional custom prompt
- [ ] Standard JSON envelope output
- [ ] Clear error codes

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Assignment state update logic
- Unit test: File reservation transfer
- Unit test: Output format validation
- E2E test: Full reassignment workflow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:36:52.859683490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:55:18.854201422Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:55:18.854147310Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gbt5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ohy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:38:38.231148699Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gbt5","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:38:24.736944643Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gbt5","depends_on_id":"bd-k3zq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:38:31.515777835Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1ghcp","title":"RCH: Implement rch binary detection and worker availability check","description":"Create utility function to detect RCH and check worker status.

## Requirements
1. Check if 'rch' binary exists on PATH
2. Verify rch version is compatible
3. Check if any workers are configured and available
4. Cache result with short TTL (workers may come/go)

## Implementation Location
Create new file: internal/integrations/rch/detect.go

```go
package rch

import (
    "encoding/json"
    "os/exec"
    "sync"
    "time"
)

type RCHStatus struct {
    Available    bool
    Version      string
    WorkerCount  int
    HealthyCount int
    LastCheck    time.Time
}

var (
    mu     sync.RWMutex
    status RCHStatus
    ttl    = 30 * time.Second
)

func GetStatus() RCHStatus {
    mu.RLock()
    if time.Since(status.LastCheck) < ttl {
        defer mu.RUnlock()
        return status
    }
    mu.RUnlock()
    
    return refresh()
}

func refresh() RCHStatus {
    mu.Lock()
    defer mu.Unlock()
    
    // Check binary
    // Run 'rch status --json'
    // Parse worker info
    status.LastCheck = time.Now()
    return status
}
```","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:07:32.155904396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:01:31.341659433Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ghcp","depends_on_id":"bd-1c9ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:07:32.180157994Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Requirements
1. Check if 'rch' binary exists on PATH
2. Verify rch version is compatible
3. Check if any workers are configured and available
4. Cache result with short TTL (workers may come/go)

## Implementation Location
Create new file: internal/integrations/rch/detect.go

```go
package rch

import (
    "encoding/json"
    "os/exec"
    "sync"
    "time"
)

type RCHStatus struct {
    Available    bool
    Version      string
    WorkerCount  int
    HealthyCount int
    LastCheck    time.Time
}

var (
    mu     sync.RWMutex
    status RCHStatus
    ttl    = 30 * time.Second
)

func GetStatus() RCHStatus {
    mu.RLock()
    if time.Since(status.LastCheck) < ttl {
        defer mu.RUnlock()
        return status
    }
    mu.RUnlock()
    
    return refresh()
}

func refresh() RCHStatus {
    mu.Lock()
    defer mu.Unlock()
    
    // Check binary
    // Run 'rch status --json'
    // Parse worker info
    status.LastCheck = time.Now()
    return status
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:07:32.155904396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:07:34.350046341Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:07:34.349997629Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: internal/tools/rch.go with RCHAdapter following the DCG adapter pattern. Includes binary detection, version checking, worker availability with 30s TTL caching. Tests in internal/tools/rch_test.go all pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ghcp","depends_on_id":"bd-1c9ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:07:32.180157994Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1gkal","title":"Implement bulk prompt delivery","description":"Implement bulk prompt delivery.

## Functionality

Send assignment prompts to multiple panes efficiently.

## Default Prompt Template

```
Read AGENTS.md, register with Agent Mail. Work on: {bead_id} - {bead_title}.
Use br show {bead_id} for details. Mark in_progress when starting. Use ultrathink.
```

## Template Variables

- {bead_id}: The bead ID
- {bead_title}: Bead title/description
- {bead_type}: task, feature, bug, etc.
- {bead_deps}: Dependencies list
- {session}: Session name
- {pane}: Pane number

## Delivery Strategy

Option A: Sequential (safer)
- Send to pane 2, wait 1s
- Send to pane 3, wait 1s
- etc.

Option B: Parallel (faster)
- Send to all panes simultaneously
- May overwhelm if too many

Recommend: Sequential by default, --parallel flag for advanced users.

## Custom Templates

--prompt-template=<FILE> for custom prompts:
```
Custom marching orders here.
Bead: {bead_id}
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:31:28.015184270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:31:28.043660276Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gkal","depends_on_id":"bd-t9qp4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:31:28.043619780Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1h22","title":"Add --distribute flag to ntm send for auto-distributing work from bv --robot-triage","description":"## Overview
Add `--distribute` flag to `ntm send` that automatically distributes work from `bv --robot-triage` to agents.

## Implementation
This is a convenience shorthand that internally calls `ntm assign`. The full workflow is:
1. Get recommendations from `bv --robot-triage`
2. Match recommendations to available agents
3. Send individualized prompts to each agent via `ntm send --pane=N`

## Usage
```bash
# Quick one-liner for distribution
ntm send myproject --distribute

# With options
ntm send myproject --distribute --limit=10 --cc-only
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --distribute flag parses correctly
- [ ] Calls bv --robot-triage internally
- [ ] Distributes N beads to N agents
- [ ] Logs which bead went to which agent
- [ ] Returns structured JSON in robot mode

## Dependencies
- Requires `ntm assign` command (bd-k3zq) as underlying implementation
- Requires `bv` integration (bd-55pm)

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Flag parsing
- Unit test: Distribution algorithm
- E2E test: Full distribute workflow with real agents","notes":"Closed as dropped: avoid extra entrypoints; use ntm assign or spawn --assign.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:31:55.360840482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:16:26.093871876Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T19:09:17.651720104Z","close_reason":"Dropped per canonical assign flow; redundant/overlapping entry points","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1h22","depends_on_id":"bd-55pm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:17:24.544795977Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1h22","depends_on_id":"bd-k3zq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:17:21.177572512Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
